mechanism_narrative: A persistently low or declining FEDFUNDS rate reduces the discount rate applied to future equity cash flows, mechanically raising present-value valuations and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days regardless of regime.
failure_condition: Rank IC between FEDFUNDS level/trend and forward 20d returns falls below 0.03 over any rolling 2-year window, or returns following low-rate periods are not statistically higher than baseline (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When UNRATE rises by more than 0.3 percentage points over a 3-month span, it signals an inflection toward labor market deterioration that precedes earnings downgrades, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in more than 55% of triggered instances, or the effect disappears (t-test p > 0.10) when tested against a non-triggered control sample across multiple cycles.

mechanism_narrative: The joint behavior of rising FEDFUNDS alongside decelerating CPIAUCSL indicates a shift from restrictive-to-neutral real rates; this combination lowers the effective real interest rate faster than nominal rates suggest, supporting positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon that a single-series read of either variable would miss.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS flat-to-rising while CPIAUCSL 6m annualized change decelerates below prior 6m average
failure_condition: Rank IC of the joint real-rate-decline signal versus forward 60d returns is not higher than the best univariate FEDFUNDS-only or CPIAUCSL-only signal across a 3-year walk-forward test.

mechanism_narrative: During late-cycle expansions, month-over-month acceleration in RSAFS reflects resilient consumer demand that supports near-term corporate revenue guidance, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days, but this relationship weakens or reverses once retail sales growth becomes a signal of overheating inflation risk in early-cycle regimes.
failure_condition: Sign of the RSAFS-forward-return relationship flips to negative and remains negative (Rank IC < -0.03) for two consecutive rolling 1-year windows within the designated regime.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained expansion in INDPRO reflects improving industrial capacity utilization and corporate output, which supports earnings growth expectations and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon independent of any specific triggering event.
failure_condition: Rank IC of INDPRO trend versus forward 60d returns falls below 0.03 across more than half of all rolling 2-year windows in the sample history.

mechanism_narrative: Positive PAYEMS month-over-month growth combined with a simultaneous decline or flatness in UNRATE signals genuine labor market tightening without slack absorption, which reinforces consumer spending capacity and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days; PAYEMS growth alone without a UNRATE confirmation is insufficient because it can reflect population growth rather than tightening.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS MoM change > 0 and UNRATE MoM change <= 0
failure_condition: Joint PAYEMS+UNRATE signal underperforms the univariate PAYEMS-only signal on Rank IC over a 3-year walk-forward test, or triggered-instance forward returns are not significantly different from non-triggered returns (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: A decline in HOUST of more than 10% from its trailing 6-month peak signals weakening housing demand that historically leads broader consumer and construction-sector slowdowns, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with rate-sensitive housing stress
failure_condition: SPX 60d forward returns following triggered HOUST declines are positive in more than 55% of instances, or Rank IC is below 0.03 over any rolling 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS exceeds 4.5% while CPIAUCSL year-over-year inflation remains elevated, markets price a higher probability of sustained restrictive policy, causing near-term risk-off pressure and negative S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days; this mechanism is specific to tightening regimes and does not apply when rates are cutting.
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with sticky inflation
failure_condition: SPX 5d forward returns are non-negative on average across triggered FEDFUNDS>4.5% instances, or the effect is not distinguishable from zero (p > 0.10) across at least two distinct tightening episodes.
